subject: [PATCH v3 2/3] drivers/firmware: add SDEI cross-CPU NMI service for arm64

2026-06-16.patch_summary: This patch adds a cross-CPU NMI service for arm64 using the SDEI (System Description Event Infrastructure) software-signalled event mechanism. The goal is to deliver an NMI-like event to an interrupt-masked CPU without the overhead of the pseudo-NMI hot-path. This is achieved by registering a handler for event 0 and signaling it with sdei_event_signal(0, mpidr). The first user of this service is arch_trigger_cpumask_backtrace(), which can now reach masked CPUs. If SDEI is absent or not enabled, the patch falls back to the IPI path. This change also adds a new file drivers/firmware/arm_sdei_nmi.c and updates several other files.
